scenes.script_line: Have you ever spent an hour doing something someone else could do in ten minutes?
scenes.visual_direction: Someone laboring slowly/inefficiently, a little frustrated, glancing at the clock.

scenes.script_line: Ask one simple question: 'Who already does this better than me?'
scenes.visual_direction: Looking to a more skilled colleague — observing, learning from someone better.

scenes.script_line: The best people in the world don't know everything. They know how to learn.
scenes.visual_direction: A curious professional actively learning — studying, taking notes.

scenes.script_line: The greatest athletes still have coaches. The greatest musicians still have teachers.
scenes.visual_direction: Coaching and mentoring — a coach guiding an athlete, a teacher and student.

scenes.script_line: Excellence leaves clues. Don't reinvent the wheel. Benchmark it.
scenes.visual_direction: Studying a proven example — analyzing a reference, a blueprint, best practice.

clips.author: Archives New Zealand from New Zealand

scenes.script_line: Maybe someone on your team writes better emails. Ask them to show you.
scenes.visual_direction: A colleague sharing a skill — showing a teammate how they do it at a screen.

scenes.script_line: Maybe AI can help. Before sending an email, ask AI: 'Can this be clearer?'
scenes.visual_direction: Using AI as a tool — a person working with an AI assistant on a laptop.

scenes.script_line: Benchmarking requires humility. Pride says 'I already know.' Humility says 'Teach me.'
scenes.visual_direction: A humble learner open to advice — asking to be taught, receptive.

scenes.script_line: Imagine two people building the same table. The second asks the best carpenter for advice.
scenes.visual_direction: Carpentry — a woodworker building a table, a mentor giving a tip in a workshop.

scenes.script_line: Ask someone who gives excellent presentations to review it. Five minutes of feedback.
scenes.visual_direction: Reviewing work together — a colleague giving quick feedback on a presentation.

scenes.script_line: Good people work hard. Great people learn fast.
scenes.visual_direction: A focused professional visibly improving — momentum, mastery building.

scenes.script_line: A culture where asking for help is normal. When one person improves, the whole team improves.
scenes.visual_direction: A team sharing knowledge and helping each other openly.

scenes.script_line: Excellence is not a destination. It's a habit. The more you learn, the more excellent you become.
scenes.visual_direction: Quiet pride in excellent, high-quality work — a polished result.
